children.heading: Claimant
children.label: 1 Claimant
children.text: The term “claimant” means a person raising a claim or defense under this chapter.

children.heading: Demonstrates
children.label: 2 Demonstrates
children.text: The term “demonstrates” means meets the burdens of going forward with the evidence and of persuasion.

children.heading: Free Exercise Clause
children.label: 3 Free Exercise Clause
children.text: The term “Free Exercise Clause” means that portion of the first amendment to the Constitution that proscribes laws prohibiting the free exercise of religion.

children.label: i
children.text: a State, county, municipality, or other governmental entity created under the authority of a State;

children.label: ii
children.text: any branch, department, agency, instrumentality, or official of an entity listed in clause (i); and

children.label: iii
children.text: any other person acting under color of State law; and

children.label: B
children.text: for the purposes of sections 2000cc–2(b) and 2000cc–3 of this title, includes the United States, a branch, department, agency, instrumentality, or official of the United States, and any other person acting under color of Federal law.

children.heading: Land use regulation
children.label: 5 Land use regulation
children.text: The term “land use regulation” means a zoning or landmarking law, or the application of such a law, that limits or restricts a claimant’s use or development of land (including a structure affixed to land), if the claimant has an ownership, leasehold, easement, servitude, or other property interest in the regulated land or a contract or option to acquire such an interest.

children.heading: Program or activity
children.label: 6 Program or activity
children.text: The term “program or activity” means all of the operations of any entity as described in paragraph (1) or (2) of .section 2000d–4a of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “religious exercise” includes any exercise of religion, whether or not compelled by, or central to, a system of religious belief.

children.heading: Rule
children.label: B Rule
children.text: The use, building, or conversion of real property for the purpose of religious exercise shall be considered to be religious exercise of the person or entity that uses or intends to use the property for that purpose.
